codeReview.summary: The feature delivers the requested dual XGBoost trainer with TimeSeriesSplit-based tuning, versioned joblib persistence, metadata/feature-importance outputs, and focused tests. I found one non-blocking artifact-versioning edge case.

issues.description: Model versions are derived from UTC timestamps with only second-level precision plus the data hash prefix. If the same dataset is trained twice within the same second, the resulting model_version will collide and silently overwrite the earlier run's .joblib, .metadata.json, and training_run_*.json artifacts (paths are built from model_version at lines 131 and 250).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The new XGBoost training CLI introduced by this feature is not discoverable from .factory/services.yaml even though that file is described as the single source of truth for useful commands. Future workers still have to rediscover the trainer entrypoint manually.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml:24-28 lists daily, daily_dryrun, backtest, and calibrate_eval, but no xgboost training command. The worker transcript/handoff shows verification used python -m src.model.xgboost_trainer --training-data ... --output-dir data/models ... directly.

summary: Pass. The implementation matches the feature requirements and the reviewed diff/tests look sound. One non-blocking issue remains around per-second artifact version collisions, and the shared command manifest does not yet advertise the new training entrypoint.
